not_applicable_exceptions.reason: The workflow removes duplicate checks and produces one commit-bound artifact; it makes no changed Agent-behavior claim.
The workflow carries the pre-registered plan as release evidence without adopting or claiming a new governance asset.
The workflow promotes the exact CI artifact and verifies its provenance; it does not change an evaluated Agent candidate.
The repository proof command is version-neutralized and remains a deterministic delivery contract with no Agent-behavior claim.
The verifier expands fail-closed sensitive-path coverage and cannot modify a candidate, runner, evaluator, or adoption decision.
The release verifier validates plan-bound fresh-pair results while preserving the legacy six-pair contract; no new candidate is adopted.
The producer replaces a fixed rollout count with a hash-bound R3 plan; this deterministic release makes no observed improvement claim.
This tracked contract binds the complete v1.11 Diff to reviewed deterministic proofs and explicitly declares no behavior adoption.
The schema describes planning-only history output and is validated without entering historical observations into a PASS decision.
The schema fail-closes verified history identity and count fields; it neither runs nor adopts an Agent candidate.
The schema records pre-execution risk, power, binding, and stopping contracts and is covered by deterministic contract tests.
Progressive disclosure shortens default context while preserving default-off activation and immutable authority boundaries exactly.
This on-demand reference relocates existing delivery guidance without granting execution, adoption, commit, push, or release authority.
This on-demand reference preserves bounded evolution and explicit human adoption; it contains no adopted candidate claim.
This on-demand reference relocates provenance commands and retains their non-executing, source-bound semantics.
This on-demand reference preserves deterministic diagnosis and validation-only regression staging without automatic repair.
This on-demand reference makes existing trust boundaries explicit and does not authorize tools or alter evaluator outcomes.
The module change is the package version declaration only and makes no claim about changed Agent behavior.
The CLI exposes explicit plan, resume, and history-assessment contracts whose routing and failure behavior are deterministically tested.
The new planner is dependency-free, pre-registered, hash-bound, and planning-only for history; it does not itself execute or adopt candidates.
The runtime removes redundant work through exact preflight, resume, reuse, and legal stopping while preserving fresh-only PASS decisions.
The statistical layer adds pre-specified directional and sequential boundaries while retaining legacy fixed-sample behavior under tests.
The merge now preserves the most conservative confidence and risk flags so duplicate evidence cannot weaken review routing.
